meaning: n.月虹(月光形成的虹,呈银色)
examples: The remarkable photograph of a 'moonbow' was taken by Wally Pacholka as he looked over the Haleakala crater on Maui Island in Hawaii.
It is possible, however, to view the colors in a moonbow using long exposure photography.
The sky must be very dark for a moonbow to be watched clearly—any bright light can obscure it.
